title: raoulia cinerea
intro: Raoulia cinerea, also known as the dust plant or dust dandelion, is a succulent plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its small, white flowers and dusty, grey-green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and skin irritations. The health benefits of Raoulia cinerea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and soothe skin conditions, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Raoulia cinerea can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
